name: Apricot Preserves
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the apricots and sugar to setting point, continually skimming off foam.
2. Shortly before reaching the setting point, add the lemon juice.
3. Remove from heat. Stir in the "Einsiedehilfe" dissolved in hot water.
4. Pour into hot, dry, sterilized jars.
5. Seal jars with cellophane, with the top of which has been dipped in rum and smooth the overhang over the jars' necks, tying with thin twine.
Makes 12 half-liter jars and one quarter-liter jar (approximately 13 to 14 pints).
"Einsiedehilfe" ('Preserving Aid') is sold in Austria in 15 gram packages and consists of 65 percent sugar and 35 percent benzoic acid.
To test for setting point: Spoon a little of the conserve onto a chilled saucer. If it doesn't run when held upside down, the setting point has been reached.
